codeReview.summary: Reviewed the prior failing report, latest handoff, commit diff, transcript skeleton, and current voice code/tests. The blocking gap is fixed: `VoicePipeline::run_transcription()` now threads `stream_audio` into `synthesize_chunk()`, and `synthesize_chunk()` records transcript text but returns before `tts_model.synthesize()` when audio streaming is disabled (`crates/agents-voice/src/pipeline.rs:77-129,388-401`). The new crate-local regression (`crates/agents-voice/src/pipeline.rs:354`) and facade regression (`crates/openai-agents/tests/voice_semantics.rs:229`) directly prove transcript-only runs succeed with zero TTS calls while the existing streamed-input, settings, and workflow-state tests still cover the rest of the feature surface.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission validation contract still cites `voice_pipeline_suppresses_audio_for_buffered_input_when_streaming_disabled` as evidence that transcript-only runs are independent from TTS, but that older test only proves audio suppression. The new exact regression that actually proves no TTS dependency is `transcript_only_runs_skip_tts_when_stream_audio_disabled`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/staticpayload/.factory/missions/a79cf586-bde6-417e-92a4-dc7681913eac/validation-contract.md:222-225 still references the older test, while /Users/staticpayload/Mainframe/openai-agents-rust/crates/openai-agents/tests/voice_semantics.rs:229 defines the new exact regression.

summary: Pass. The re-review confirms the prior blocking issue is resolved: transcript-only voice runs with `stream_audio=false` now skip TTS entirely instead of merely filtering emitted audio afterward, and the changed surface still covers streamed input, settings forwarding, and workflow continuity. I found no remaining blocking or non-blocking code issues in the reviewed surface; the only follow-up worth noting is that the mission validation contract should point at the new exact no-TTS regression.
